title: hieracium rectum subsp. neodipsaceum
intro: Hieracium rectum subsp. neodipsaceum, also known as a subspecies of hawkweed, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating digestive issues, due to its high concentration of flavonoids, saponins, and other bioactive compounds. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of Hieracium rectum subsp. neodipsaceum are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
